steps.title: Sketch two gesture lines (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch two gestures side by side: a tall near-vertical for the person, and a lower, more horizontal gesture line for the dog ahead. Place the dog slightly forward and below — it's pulling.

steps.title: Connect them with the leash (pencil)
steps.description: Draw a diagonal line from the person's hand level down to the dog's collar area. A taut leash is nearly straight; a relaxed one bows in a gentle curve. Decide which mood this walk has.

steps.title: Block in the person (pencil)
steps.description: Rough in the person: a small oval for the head, a torso wedge, and the legs — mid-stride or planted, depending on whether the dog is pulling hard. The arm holding the leash extends slightly forward.

steps.title: Block in the dog (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch the dog as a simple horizontal oval for the body, four short leg lines below, a head shape at the front, and a tail suggestion at the back. The dog's body leans forward — its nose leads.

steps.title: Check the leash connection points (pencil)
steps.description: Mark where the leash leaves the person's hand and where it reaches the dog's neck. Notice if the leash crosses in front of or behind any leg — that overlap is a key detail for making the scene feel real.

steps.title: Switch to ink and ink the person
steps.description: Switch to ink. Ink the person first — head, torso, legs, and the arm extending toward the leash. Where a leg or the arm crosses in front of the body, stop the body's outline at that crossing and restart it on the other side.

steps.title: Ink the dog and leash (ink)
steps.description: Ink the dog — body oval, four legs, head, tail. Keep it loose; the dog is at a distance. Then ink the leash as a single clean stroke between the two figures. Where the leash passes in front of a leg, stop the leg line at the crossing and restart it below.
